sections.heading: Configure Session Timeouts
sections.body:
Organization Owners can configure the absolute and idle session timeouts for all users of
an organization to comply with security and certification policies.

The **absolute session timeout** specifies the maximum duration a user can remain
logged in to the Atlas UI without re-authenticating. The **idle session timeout**
specifies the maximum duration a user can remain inactive before Atlas logs them out.

> **Note:** This feature is in Public Preview. The feature and the corresponding documentation might change at any time during the Preview period. To learn more, see [Preview Features](preview-features).

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
- If a user belongs to multiple organizations, their session inherits the shortest
  idle and absolute timeouts from across all of them. For example, a user's session
  combines the shortest absolute timeout from one organization with the shortest
  idle timeout from another. To view the timeouts that apply to you,
  see the [Personalization page](atlas-personalization).

- Two minutes before an idle session expires, Atlas displays a message allowing
  the user to continue the session.

- Timeout configurations apply only to the Atlas UI. They do not affect:

  - account access through API keys or service accounts.

  - sessions where users log in to developer tools like [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass), the Relational Migrator,
    or the Atlas CLI using their Atlas credentials. A fixed 12-hour session
    timeout applies instead of the custom values.

  - sessions where users log in through MongoDB Charts and Atlas App Services directly.

  - MongoDB SSO-linked services such as MongoDB University and the Support Portal.
    These services use a 12-hour session timeout.

  > **Important:** For the tools and services listed above, sessions might still expire in less than
  > 12 hours if your main Atlas web session ends.

sections.heading: Step 1: Set the absolute and idle session timeouts.
sections.body:
- In the **Configure Session Timeout** section,
  click pencil.

- Specify the absolute and idle session timeouts.

  The absolute session timeout must be greater than or equal to the idle
  session timeout.

  For Atlas, limits include:

  | Session Timeout | Default  | Minimum   | Maximum                  |
  | --------------- | -------- | --------- | ------------------------ |
  | Absolute        | 12 hours | 1 hour    | 12 hours                 |
  | Idle            | None     | 5 minutes | Absolute session timeout |

  For MongoDB Atlas for Government, limits include:

  | Session Timeout | Default    | Minimum   | Maximum    |
  | --------------- | ---------- | --------- | ---------- |
  | Absolute        | 12 hours   | 1 hour    | 12 hours   |
  | Idle            | 10 minutes | 5 minutes | 10 minutes |

- Click **Save**.

  The settings apply to the user's next log in.

sections.heading: Add Security Contact Information
sections.body: Specify a designated Atlas security contact to receive security-related notifications.

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
> **Important:** Don't add a user as a security contact within 30 days of deleting
> their user account. We recommend that you use a distribution list
> instead of an individual email address to help ensure consistent
> communication.

- You can specify only one email address to receive security-related
  notifications including those from the MongoDB Security Team.

- We recommend that you create a mailing list that uses a single
  address, and specify it as the security contact as a best practice.
  A mailing list can also send these notifications to multiple users.

- Specifying a security contact does not grant them authorization or
  access to Atlas for security decisions or approvals.

- If the account for the security contact gets deleted, the
  security contact setting is cleared, and the former security
  contact is shown as the modifier on the related notifications and
  events.

sections.heading: Step 1: Specify an  security contact.
sections.body:
- In the **Atlas Security Contact Information** section,
  click pencil.

- Specify an email address.

  Specifying a valid email address enables the **Save**
  button.

- Click **Save**.

sections.heading: Delete an Existing Security Contact
sections.body: To delete the currently specified security contact:

sections.heading: Step 1: Specify an  security contact.
sections.body:
- In the **Atlas Security Contact Information** section,
  click pencil.

- Delete the existing email address.

- Click **Save**.

sections.heading: Disable Generative AI Features
sections.body:
Generative AI features are enabled by default in Atlas.
When this setting is turned on, [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)
can enable or disable individual AI features at the project level.
To learn more, see [Generative AI FAQs](generative-ai-faq).

To disable generative AI features for all projects in your Atlas organization:

sections.heading: Step 1: Toggle the button next to  to .
sections.body:
This disables Atlas features that use generative AI across
all projects in the organization.

sections.heading: Integrations
sections.body:
You can configure Atlas for the following integrations:

- [Slack](add-slack-to-organization)

- [Vercel](add-vercel-pointer-only)

sections.heading: Configure Slack Workspaces
sections.body:
- Type your workspace name into the field.

- Click Continue.

- Select your authentication method.

- Click Authorize.

> **Important:** You can authorize only one Slack Workspace for each Organization.

sections.heading: Select Organization to Remove Slack Authorization
sections.body:
> **Important:** This does not stop Atlas from sending alerts. To stop Atlas
> from sending alerts to Slack, you must [delete the Slack alert setting](delete-alert-setting).

sections.heading: Configure Vercel Integrations
sections.body:
You can connect your Atlas clusters to applications that you
deploy using Vercel. To learn more, see [Integrate with Vercel](vercel).
